behaviors.behavior: AC1 a real producer record maps all five types with real counts and per-finding detail
behaviors.evidence: review-record.test.ts 'a record the producer actually wrote' (5 cases, driven by the verbatim fixture) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC2 self is present, first, and labelled in plain language
behaviors.evidence: review-record.test.ts contract-order case + MissionSlice2Details.test.tsx 'labels the self-review' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC3 not_run and not_applicable stay distinct and render their disposition
behaviors.evidence: review-record.test.ts 2 status cases + MissionSlice2Details 'did not APPLY' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC4 a pending type renders unavailable with a null count, never as clean
behaviors.evidence: review-record.test.ts 'renders a still-pending type as unavailable' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC5 absent record falls back to the marker path unchanged, internal note intact
behaviors.evidence: review-state.precedence.test.ts 'no record — the pre-record behaviour, unchanged' (2) + the 12 pre-existing review-state tests PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC6 a corrupt or foreign record is an integrity fault that does NOT fall back and is SHOWN
behaviors.evidence: review-state.precedence.test.ts 'a corrupt record is a fault' (3) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC7 an unstructured pass shows no count, in the row AND in the summary
behaviors.evidence: MissionSlice2Details 'NEVER shows 0 issues' + precedence 'summary never says raised no issues' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC8 a null severity is rendered without inventing one
behaviors.evidence: review-record.test.ts 'does not invent a severity' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC9 the record is read through isSafeRunId + pathGuard + realPathGuard and a 2 MB bound; ENOENT alone is absence
behaviors.evidence: review-record.validation.test.ts (12 cases incl. unsafe run id) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC10 the client mirror stays byte-aligned with the server SoT
behaviors.evidence: mission-context-types-sync.test.ts PASSED (it correctly FAILED until ReviewParseStatus + ReviewSource were registered)

behaviors.behavior: A record naming a different run is refused rather than shown as this run's history
behaviors.evidence: review-record.validation.test.ts + precedence 'a record naming a different run' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: An all-pending record keeps the artifact not_yet_created instead of claiming no review ran
behaviors.evidence: review-state.precedence.test.ts 'a freshly materialized record is not a review history' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: An unsafe run id is reported as unidentifiable, not as a corrupt record
behaviors.evidence: review-state.precedence.test.ts 'an unsafe run id is identified as such' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: parse_status partial presents its count as a floor, never as complete
behaviors.evidence: MissionSlice2Details 'presents a PARTIAL parse as a floor' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A capped finding list is disclosed rather than implying completeness
behaviors.evidence: MissionSlice2Details 'discloses a capped finding list' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Schema faults: unknown parse_status, missing disposition, unstructured-with-findings
The finding spans are visually separated by CSS, which jsdom cannot verify
behaviors.evidence: mission-artifacts-s2.spec.ts toHaveCSS(display,flex)+(flex-direction,column) in a REAL browser PASSED

behaviors.behavior: The marker-fallback E2E still represents every pass explicitly
behaviors.evidence: mission-artifacts-s2.spec.ts 'Review shows the five passes' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC1/AC7 end-to-end: a seeded per-run record renders the self row, a per-finding location, a did-not-apply reason, and NO count for an unitemizable pass
behaviors.evidence: client/e2e/flows/mission-artifacts-s2.spec.ts 'Review reads the per-run record' — real browser against an isolated stack, 5 passed

behaviors.behavior: Rendering of a record produced by a future producer schema version
behaviors.reason: A version this reader does not understand is refused as an integrity fault, which IS tested ('rejects a schema version it does not understand'). What a hypothetical v2 should render cannot be tested before v2 exists; the fail-closed behaviour is the whole contract until then.
